name: Enable/Disable
description: A control behavior that activates, deactivates, or toggles the state of game objects and their attached behaviors during runtime. Acts as a state management system that responds to events from other behaviors, enabling dynamic object visibility, behavior activation, and performance optimization through selective component control.
documentation:
## Enable/Disable
Control the active state of objects and behaviors at runtime.

### Action
- **Enable** - Activate the target
- **Disable** - Deactivate the target
- **Toggle** - Switch the current state

### Target Type
- **Game Object** - Affect the entire object (visibility + all behaviors)
- **Specific Behaviors** - Only affect selected behaviors on an object

### Tips
- Defaults to **Start on Trigger** (designed for use with Trigger)
- Use Toggle for on/off switches (lights, doors, traps)
- Target specific behaviors to selectively disable functionality
- Great for puzzle mechanics and dynamic environments
author: stemstudio

startOnTrigger.name: Start on Trigger
startOnTrigger.description: When enabled, the action only executes after receiving a trigger event from another behavior instead of running immediately on start.

action.name: Action
action.description: Whether to activate, deactivate, or switch the target's current state

targetType.name: Target Type
targetType.description: Choose to modify the entire game object or specific behaviors

targetObject.name: Target Object
targetObject.description: The game object whose state will be modified

targetObjectBehaviors.name: Target Object Behaviors
targetObjectBehaviors.description: The game object and its specific behaviors whose state will be modified
